judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with essentially equivalent substance; Report 2 is slightly more precise (e.g., correctly locating infra/main.tf's lack of a gate and citing exact reminders.py stub lines), but the difference is marginal.

report_1.evidence: An existing clinic that has never opened this setting WILL have reminders sent automatically.** The deciding code is `auto_send_reminders_enabled` in `backend/app/feature_flags.py`, specifically lines 11–12: ... if tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders") is None: return True

report_2.evidence: A clinic that has never opened the setting WILL have reminders sent automatically.** The deciding code is `auto_send_reminders_enabled` in `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py`, specifically lines 11–12: ... if tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders") is None: return True

report_1.evidence: I would change `backend/app/feature_flags.py:12` to `return False` (making missing/unset mean disabled)

report_2.evidence: I would change `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-13` so that `None` (and ideally any missing/malformed value) returns `False`

report_1.evidence: Commit `25509a7` (SLOT-190) created `compact_calendar_view_enabled` with the `None → True` pattern — reasonable for a cosmetic calendar-view preference where you want the nicer view by default. Commit `aa4cf8d` (SLOT-214) then added `auto_send_reminders_enabled` as a line-for-line copy of that function with only the key name changed

report_2.evidence: It was copy-pasted from the calendar-view resolver, and that was not a sound basis.** Commit `aa4cf8d` ("Add automatic appointment reminders (SLOT-214)") shows `auto_send_reminders_enabled` was added directly beneath `compact_calendar_view_enabled` (created earlier in `25509a7`, SLOT-190) with byte-for-byte identical structure ... The two flags are in opposite risk categories, so the missing-value semantics needed to be re-decided

report_1.evidence: Check the blast radius of the job**: `reminders.py:24` iterates *every* tenant on every run with no per-tenant kill switch beyond this flag and no global feature gate. I'd want a global environment-level switch (or staged rollout) in addition to the per-clinic toggle, so first enablement doesn't fan out to all clinics at once.

report_2.evidence: Add an env-level master switch** ANDed with the per-tenant value (e.g. `REMINDERS_ENABLED`, default off), so the whole feature can be killed in one place without a per-tenant data migration. Nothing like this exists: `infra/main.tf` defines only `environment`, and the job's only gate is the per-tenant flag.
